macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Trump Rejects Iran's Response to US Peace Proposal, Oil Prices Surge
items.summary: President Trump rejected Iran's response to a US peace proposal, which demanded compensation and sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz, raising fears the 10-week conflict will continue and paralyze shipping, sending oil prices higher.

items.title: ECB Expected to Hike Rates by 50bps
items.summary: The European Central Bank is anticipated to raise rates by 50 basis points in its upcoming decision, with a press conference to follow, amid ongoing economic data releases.

items.title: US Mortgage Rates Rise to 6.45%
items.summary: The US MBA 30-year mortgage rate increased to 6.45% as of May 1, reflecting pressures from inflation and geopolitical tensions.

items.title: EU PPI Beats Expectations at 2.1% YoY
items.summary: Eurozone Producer Prices rose 2.1% year-over-year in March, exceeding forecasts of 1.8%, signaling persistent inflationary pressures.

items.title: Wall Street Mixed Amid AI Earnings and Inflation Risks
items.summary: US markets faced persistent 3.3% CPI inflation and geopolitical tensions during May 4-10, with Fed holding rates at 3.50%-3.75% despite dissent; tech stocks resilient on AI-driven earnings.

items.title: Lavrov and Rubio Discuss Russia-US Relations
items.summary: Russian Foreign Minister Lavrov and US Senator Rubio discussed international agenda, bilateral relations, and future contacts, per Russian reports.

items.title: Stocks Climb on Strong April Jobs Data
items.summary: US stocks rose as April jobs growth exceeded estimates, with unemployment steady at 4.3%; Q1 GDP grew 2.0%.

items.title: Fed Keeps Rates Unchanged in Powell's Swan Song
items.summary: The Federal Reserve maintained rates at 3.50%-3.75% following its April meeting, with dissent on inflation concerns.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Futures Liquidations Reach $86.63 Million in 24 Hours, Mostly Shorts
items.summary: Total cryptocurrency futures liquidations hit $86.63 million across the network, with $56.9 million from short positions and $29.7 million from longs; BTC and ETH saw $16.88 million and $12.63 million liquidated respectively.

items.title: Crypto Derivatives Market Sees $218 Million in Liquidations Over 24 Hours
items.summary: Significant volatility led to $218 million in futures liquidations, led by Bitcoin at $91.49 million and Ethereum at $43.98 million, with Solana and XRP also notable.

items.title: Crypto Market Cap Hits $2.68 Trillion with 1.39% Gain Amid Neutral Sentiment
items.summary: Total crypto market capitalization rose to $2.68T, up 1.39%, though trading volume dropped 51% to $95.35B; Fear & Greed Index at 50 (Neutral). Top gainers include BPX (+1929%), GPM (+1674%), and NFLXX (+792%).

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Above $81,000 as Market Rises Generally
items.summary: Bitcoin rose 1.17% to above $81,000, with XRP up 3.29%, TWT 6.55%, and XEC 12.85%, amid a broader market uptrend.

items.title: Bitcoin Nears $83,400 Amid Inflation Data and Fed Uncertainty
items.summary: BTC surged past $81,000 toward $83,400 driven by institutional demand, ETF inflows, and upcoming CPI/PPI data that could influence Fed policies.

items.title: Bitcoin Spot ETFs Record $623 Million Net Inflows for Sixth Straight Week
items.summary: Bitcoin ETFs saw $623M inflows last week; Ethereum ETFs $70.49M with BlackRock's ETHA at $100M, SOL ETF $39.23M, and XRP ETF $34.21M.

items.title: SUI Price Jumps 31%, Trends with SWEAT and ZANO
items.summary: SUI surged 31% over the weekend to above $1.40, joining CoinGecko's trending list and signaling renewed altcoin interest.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Canvas Learning Platform Suffers Major Data Breach Affecting 275 Million Users
items.summary: Instructure's Canvas learning management platform experienced a cyberattack by ShinyHunters, affecting approximately 9,000 schools and over 275 million individuals including students, teachers, and staff. The breach disrupted finals week for thousands of users worldwide.

items.title: Synchron Achieves First Native Brain-Computer Interface Integration with Apple Devices
items.summary: Synchron announced the first native BCI (brain-computer interface) integration with iPhone, iPad, and Apple Vision Pro, marking a significant milestone in brain-computer interface technology accessibility.

items.title: UCSF Researchers Achieve Sub-80 Millisecond Brain-to-Speech Translation
items.summary: UC San Francisco scientists broke records in 2025 by developing AI that can translate brain signals to speech in less than 80 milliseconds—faster than a human blink. The technology enables ALS patients to communicate through digital avatars with facial expression recognition.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Atari Acquires Rights to First Five Wizardry Games
items.summary: Atari has made a significant retro gaming move by acquiring rights to the first five Wizardry RPG titles, potentially building a larger presence in the classic IP space. The news is part of a weekly roundup including other retro developments like Super Mario 64 on Game Boy Advance and delays for the THEA1200 Amiga-style computer.

items.title: THEA1200 Amiga-Style Retro Computer Delayed Again
items.summary: Retro Games Limited has announced another delay for the THEA1200, their upcoming plug-and-play system based on the original Omega 1200.

items.title: Sony Records $565 Million Impairment Loss on Bungie
items.summary: Sony's latest earnings report revealed a $565 million impairment loss against Bungie, amid declining player counts for Marathon and Destiny 2 on life support throughout 2026.

items.title: New Leaks Reveal Steam Machine with 4 Different SKUs and Reservation System
items.summary: Fresh leaks about the upcoming Steam Machine show plans for four different models and a reservation system similar to Steam Deck.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Nintendo Raising Switch 2 Prices Amid Chip Crisis
items.summary: Nintendo is increasing Switch 2 prices as chip supply challenges continue, despite selling 19.86 million units last year and forecasting lower sales next year.

items.title: GM Settles California Lawsuit Over Misuse of Driving Data
items.summary: General Motors agreed to pay $12.75 million to settle a lawsuit over collecting driving data through OnStar and selling it to data brokers.

items.title: Samsung's Bespoke AI Update Brings Smart Features to Refrigerators
items.summary: Samsung released a software upgrade for its Bespoke refrigerators, marking a significant step toward making AI more practical for household appliances.

items.title: Intel Signs Preliminary Deal to Produce Chips for Apple
items.summary: Intel has reportedly signed a preliminary agreement to manufacture chips for Apple, marking a potential shift in Apple's chip supply strategy.

items.title: Boox Announces Tappy Wireless Page-Turning Remote
items.summary: Boox introduced Tappy, a wireless page-turning remote that works with e-readers and other Bluetooth devices.

items.title: FCC Allows Critical Updates for Banned Drones and Routers Until 2029
items.summary: The Federal Communications Commission issued a notice permitting software and firmware updates for banned drones and routers until January 2029.
